proof: real-model OUTCOME matrix — a real model drives an OpenContext flow to fix a bug and pass its test
method: scripts/real_model_outcome.sh: isolated HOME+project (buggy add + failing test) -> opencontext setup <host> -> host drives opencontext_run(<workflow>) -> agent_execute -> session_apply -> verify fix landed AND pytest passes

codex.model: gpt-5.5 (ChatGPT-account default; nano/mini/codex rejected by that account)
codex.note: needed --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox: this box's bubblewrap cannot create user namespaces

claude.note: SDD strict+off = full flow + fix; oc-flow/strict fixed but edited directly (flow bypass); oc-flow/off engaged flow but weakest model did not land the fix

verdict: SDD (with and without TDD) proven end-to-end on all three real hosts with real models. OC Flow proven on opencode + codex; flaky on claude/haiku (cheapest model). Reliability scales with model capability, not with the product plumbing.
